Content: [Piano plays Mozart's Piano Sonata No 11] McConnell: This is the first music I remember in my life, hearing.
Content_zh: [鋼琴演奏莫札特第 11 號鋼琴奏鳴曲] McConnell：這是我人生中有記憶以來，第一次聽到的音樂。

Content: [Music] McConnell: So it's the Swiss side of me there.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：所以這就是我瑞士血統的那一面。

Content: It's just really great to be able to sit down and play something without turning it on.
Content_zh: 能坐下來，不用開機就演奏一樣東西，真的很棒。。

Content: Makes music.
Content_zh: 它會發出音樂。

Content: [Music] Hansen: I get really nervous around cameras, it's my thing.
Content_zh: [音樂] Hansen：我在鏡頭前會很緊張，這是我的問題。

Content: All right.
Content_zh: 好吧

Content: Hansen: Yeah, Shellmount first pass.
Content_zh: Hansen：對， Shellmound 第一版。

Content: Dead Eye God first pass.
Content_zh: Dead Eye God 第一版。

Content: Lumberjack first pass.
Content_zh: 伐木工第一版。

Content: Localization QA starts.
Content_zh: 在地化 QA 開始。

Content: Rice: Live instrument recording.
Content_zh: Rice：現場樂器錄音。

Content: Liu: Yeah, VO play-testing and testing.
Content_zh: Liu：對， VO 試玩測試和測試。

Content: Rice: This is the big one.
Content_zh: Rice：這是最大的一個。

Content: Liu: Oh, you're putting it in all caps?
Content_zh: Liu：喔，你要全部用大寫？

Content: Rice: Of course. There it is.
Content_zh: Rice：當然 找到了

Content: Schafer: Do you want to talk about schedule stuff before we begin the meeting?
Content_zh: Schafer：你想在我們開會前談一下時程嗎？

Content: Rice: Sure. Very exciting stuff.
Content_zh: Rice：好 非常刺激的事

Content: Schafer: Greg's got exciting schedule stuff to talk about.
Content_zh: Schafer：Greg 有很刺激的時程

Content: Rice: OK. Well, today's end of a sprint, right?
Content_zh: Rice：好，今天是一個 sprint 的結尾，對吧？

Content: So we should talk about this in light of that.
Content_zh: 所以我們應該在這個前提下談。

Content: But yeah, Matt and Sue and I have been working on schedule stuff for the project for a while now.
Content_zh: 是的，Matt 和 Sue 和我 已經做了一陣子計劃的時程了

Content: So we have a lot of data that I guess we'd talked through.
Content_zh: 所以我們有很多資料 我想我們談過了

Content: But I guess first we should probably just walk through how all this stuff happened, to explain where we came from.
Content_zh: 但我想我們應該先把整件事怎麼走到這裡講一遍，解釋一下來龍去脈。

Content: Bailey: And the thought is, is that we potentially have scope-wise, enough content for a game to ship sooner rather than later.
Content_zh: Bailey：想法是，以範圍來看，我們可能已經有足夠內容，可以讓遊戲早點推出。

Content: Schafer: January for Part One and April for Part Two.
Content_zh: Schafer：第一部一月，第二部四月。

Content: Schafer: As long as it's definitely just Part One and you're definitely going to get Part Two.
Content_zh: Schafer：只要這真的只是第一部，而且你確定之後會拿到第二部。

Content: It's not just, because I hated the end of Empire.
Content_zh: 這不是單純因為我討厭《帝國大反擊》的結尾。

Content: I mean, when I was a kid and they ended that way, I was like what the fuck?
Content_zh: 我是說，我小時候看到那樣收尾，心想這他媽什麼鬼？

Content: Rice: So that's how we got to this.
Content_zh: Rice：所以我們就是這樣走到這一步的。

Content: So this is kind of where that is for Part One right now and then there's also Part Two down here.
Content_zh: 所以這大概就是第一部目前的狀況，下面也有第二部。

Content: But it kind of gives you a good snapshot as far as what the next year would look like to hit that January date.
Content_zh: 不過這能讓你很清楚看到，為了趕上一月日期，接下來一年大概會長什麼樣子。

Content: Schafer: And even this is pretty scary when you look at just two weeks, total for Dead Eye God Pyramid.
Content_zh: Schafer：光看 Dead Eye God Pyramid 總共只有兩週，這就已經很可怕了。

Content: Like us all together working on that and banging that whole room out.
Content_zh: 像是我們所有人一起做那段，把整個房間趕出來。

Content: The interior and the exterior in two weeks, right? Rice: Yep.
Content_zh: 內部和外部都要在兩週內，對吧？Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: Has that been proven to work by January? Or April?
Content_zh: Schafer：這真的已經證明一月前可行了嗎？還是四月？

Content: Rice: Yeah, I mean as far as, like as accurate as we can get.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我是說，就我們能估到的準確程度來看。

Content: Like we're, Schafer: You did already or...
Content_zh: 像我們…… Schafer：你們已經做過了，還是……

Content: Hansen: We quantified that. Schafer: OK.
Content_zh: Hansen：我們量化過了。Schafer：好。

Content: Rice: Yeah. We went through and like assigned what each of those scenes, how complex they are compared to an average scene, which we called the Galley.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我們把那些場景一個個看過，評估它們相對於平均場景有多複雜；那個平均場景我們叫 Galley。

Content: And ran the numbers on all of it and that's how we figured out like how many scenes and what type we can get.
Content_zh: 然後把數字都算過，才估出我們能做多少場景、哪些類型。

Content: So there's a little bit of fudging there, like if you have scenes that are super-complex,
Content_zh: 所以這裡有一點估算空間，例如如果場景超複雜，

Content: that's going to count as more than something that's a hallway.
Content_zh: 它就會比走廊之類的東西算更多。

Content: Schafer: It also shows the consequences of if we don't get that Dead Eye done in two weeks.
Content_zh: Schafer：這也顯示，如果 Dead Eye 不能在兩週內完成，後果會是什麼。

Content: You see there's no other room for it to go into expect for the push.
Content_zh: 你看，除了往後推，沒有其他地方能塞。

Content: Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: Push all the other dates out, which would be a huge problem because the deadline in January
Content_zh: Schafer：把其他日期全往後推，這會是大問題，因為一月的期限

Content: is a revenue-based deadline, basically that Justin says he needs that revenue in January or…
Content_zh: 基本上是跟收入有關的期限， Justin 說他一月需要那筆收入，不然……

Content: Bailey: The reason the game must be done in January is because that's at the time we've run out of funds for that game.
Content_zh: Bailey：遊戲一定要在一月完成，是因為到那時我們這款遊戲的資金就用完了。

Content: We're taking all the funds the studio makes and putting it into Broken Age.
Content_zh: 我們把工作室賺到的資金全都投進《Broken Age》。

Content: Like all of them.
Content_zh: 真的是全部。

Content: So, but it becomes, in January, this comes this point in time where floating that big of a team on an ongoing basis
Content_zh: 所以到了一月，就會到一個時間點，繼續養這麼大的團隊，

Content: just can't be done out of our own royalties and our own profits of the studio.
Content_zh: 光靠工作室自己的權利金和利潤，已經撐不下去。

Content: Like it breaks at that time.
Content_zh: 那時候就會斷掉。

Content: So yeah, we just, we have to ship something and have to start making some money off that project.
Content_zh: 所以對，我們必須推出一些東西，開始從這個專案賺到一點錢。

Content: Peck: It's a little surprising that we have four weeks for spaceship Beta, given how far we've taken it.
Content_zh: Peck：考慮到我們已經做到這個程度，太空船 Beta 還有四週有點令人意外。

Content: Rice: Yeah, and I think so, too.
Content_zh: Rice：我也這麼想。

Content: It's weird when you look at that stuff, but the reasons are like if you look at this list,
Content_zh: 你看那些東西會覺得奇怪，但原因是，如果你看這張清單，

Content: the amount of scenes in the spaceship are pretty much equal to the amount of scenes in the entire girl's world.
Content_zh: 太空船裡的場景數量，幾乎等於整個女孩世界的場景數量。

Content: And you know there is, Lee: Animation.
Content_zh: 而且你知道，還有…… Lee：動畫。

Content: I mean you got to do all the final animation.
Content_zh: 我是說，所有最終動畫都得做。

Content: Rice: All the animation, Lee: a lot of cut scenes.
Content_zh: Rice：所有動畫。Lee：還有很多過場動畫。

Content: Schafer: And the thing about that is, is that this, because this is so tight and uncomfortable and there's not a big space for like,
Content_zh: Schafer：問題是，因為這個時程很緊、很不舒服，也沒有太多空間給，

Content: there's some play-test feedback in here but it's not like a month of just play-test feedback or two,
Content_zh: 這裡有一些試玩測試回饋，但不是我們真正想要的那種，整整一兩個月只處理試玩回饋。

Content: which is what we'd really like.
Content_zh: 這才是我們真正想要的。

Content: So I think we're always going to have to be looking at places where we're finishing some things early
Content_zh: 所以我想我們總得找個地方 早點完成一些事情

Content: and if that four weeks sounds like a lot of time, that maybe hopefully it means that the game play programmers
Content_zh: 如果那四週聽起來很多，也許希望這代表 gameplay 程式設計師

Content: could get ahead to create that buffer where we actually do have like a month to play-test feedback.
Content_zh: 能先往前趕，創造出緩衝，讓我們真的有大概一個月可以處理試玩回饋。

Content: That would be awesome.
Content_zh: 那就太棒了。

Content: Schafer: The schedule's done and I want it to be a little more like a home run
Content_zh: Schafer：時程排好了，但我希望它更像是穩贏的全壘打，

Content: than it is, because it's kind of like yeah, it looks on paper like we can do it,
Content_zh: 而不是現在這樣；因為它有點像，紙面上看起來我們做得到，

Content: but there's not a lot of wiggle room.
Content_zh: 但沒有太多迴旋空間。

Content: And normally you want a lot of buffer time because things always do go wrong and take longer than you thought.
Content_zh: 通常你會想要很多緩衝時間，因為事情總是會出錯，也會比你想的更久。

Content: So I think we just have a little more work to do on it to make sure we have enough buffer room in it.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得我們還得再調一下，確保裡面有足夠緩衝。

Content: Or we have to go along every time we get something done, try and get it done a little early.
Content_zh: 或者我們得每完成一件事，都試著提早一點完成。

Content: But that's always kind of like dreaming.
Content_zh: 但那總是有點像做夢。

Content: Like every project, you want to do that.
Content_zh: 每個專案都會想這樣。

Content: Like, oh, let's just get everything done a little bit early and then we'll build up a buffer time.
Content_zh: 像是，喔，我們就把所有東西都提早一點做完，然後累積緩衝時間。

Content: But I haven't, haven't ever seen that really happen.
Content_zh: 但我從來沒看過那真的發生。

Content: Rice: Do we have other thoughts?
Content_zh: Rice：還有其他想法嗎？

Content: Questions?
Content_zh: 有問題嗎？

Content: This thing's all alive so it's really easy to plug numbers in it and change them around and see where it goes,
Content_zh: 這東西都是活的，所以很容易把數字放進去、改來改去，看結果會往哪走。

Content: Schafer: I think the, doing early access for the game impacts Oliver the most.
Content_zh: Schafer：我覺得幫遊戲做 Early Access，受影響最大的是 Oliver。

Content: Because his stuff has to be done.
Content_zh: 因為他的東西一定得完成。

Content: His system work has to be done in January.
Content_zh: 他的系統工作一月就必須完成。

Content: He's got to have a shippable product in January, which means all the, there's a lot of like stuff that, you know,
Content_zh: 他一月就得有可以推出的產品，也就是說，很多那種你知道的東西，

Content: you tend to have pushed off to the end of the project, just the kind of like, maybe it's optimization
Content_zh: 通常會被推到專案最後才做，像是最佳化，

Content: or just wrapping things up to make sure that they're airtight.
Content_zh: 或是收尾，確保東西都萬無一失。

Content: You know, that all has to happen a lot earlier.
Content_zh: 那些都得更早發生。

Content: And that's probably hardest on him.
Content_zh: 這對他大概最辛苦。

Content: Rice: Yeah. I mean, I guess the only thing is just, just know going into starting on Monday,
Content_zh: Rice：對。我的意思是，唯一要知道的是，週一開始時，

Content: we're spending three months on Sugar Bunting and then, three weeks...
Content_zh: 我們要花三個月做 Sugar Bunting，然後三週……

Content: Schafer: Three months! Three months!
Content_zh: Schafer：三個月！三個月！

Content: Rice: God, I wish.
Content_zh: Rice：天啊，我也希望是。

Content: No, three weeks on Sugar Bunting and then moving on, so.
Content_zh: 不，是 Sugar Bunting 三週，然後就繼續往下做。

Content: I mean, you can see why.
Content_zh: 我是說，你看得出原因。

Content: Schafer: OK. I wrote a thing explaining the early access stuff to the backers and we'll do an update on Monday.
Content_zh: Schafer：好。我寫了一篇文章，向贊助者解釋 Early Access 的事，我們週一會更新。

Content: That is, a full-on update with the episode on Monday?No.
Content_zh: 也就是說，週一會是完整更新加上那集嗎？不。

Content: Rice: No. Episode will be Tuesday; I think you're just going to post in the forums, like.
Content_zh: Rice：不。那集會週二上；我想你只是會在論壇發文。

Content: Schafer: So when is Chris writing an update, on Tuesday.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以 Chris 是週二寫更新？

Content: Schafer: And that's the one that's attached to the episode.
Content_zh: Schafer：那篇才會附在該集旁邊。

Content: Schafer: In Red's this week we're having episode ten go out live, which is the one where we're talking our shipping plan.
Content_zh: Schafer：Red’s 這週會讓第十集上線，就是我們談推出計畫的那集。

Content: And so that could be very active, a very active week for the backers.
Content_zh: 所以對贊助者來說，那可能會是非常熱鬧的一週。

Content: Active in just how much they love the new plan, talking about that is what I mean.
Content_zh: 我是說，熱鬧在於他們多麼喜歡新計畫，大家會一直聊這件事。

Content: And then, Team Member: Is it going out today?
Content_zh: 然後…… 團隊成員：今天會上線嗎？

Content: Schafer: Tomorrow.
Content_zh: Schafer：明天。

Content: Schafer: OK, here's my post. Long post.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，這是我的貼文。很長的一篇。

Content: I like the first thing; sounds great.
Content_zh: 我喜歡第一個回應，聽起來很棒。。

Content: And so the wait for January begins.
Content_zh: 所以一月的等待開始了

Content: What a pleasant fellow.
Content_zh: 真是個和善的人。

Content: And what would we need the next one, mean and horrible.
Content_zh: 那下一個我們需要什麼，刻薄又可怕的嗎？

Content: Sounds OK with me, chief.
Content_zh: 我覺得可以，老大。

Content: See, like they're talking to me.
Content_zh: 看，好像他們在跟我說話

Content: People are pretty polite.
Content_zh: 人們很禮貌

Content: Yeah, see all these people are really positive about it.
Content_zh: 對，看這些人都很樂觀

Content: I don't want to scroll down any farther in case someone says something mean.
Content_zh: 我不想再往下捲，免得有人講難聽話。

Content: But let's see how long is this thread.
Content_zh: 但來看看這串有多長。

Content: This is like three pages now, four, five.
Content_zh: 現在大概三頁了，四、五頁。

Content: Schafer: We announced the new plan for releasing the game on our forums and our backers are pretty great about it.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們在論壇公布了遊戲的新發行計畫，贊助者的反應其實很棒。。

Content: I mean, they want to talk about it.
Content_zh: 我是說，他們想討論這件事。

Content: They were, you know; want to make sure it was going to be a good thing.
Content_zh: 他們想確認這會是一件好事。

Content: And I think we had a pretty good reaction to it, which is good, because I would like to have this be locked and settled
Content_zh: 我覺得反應相當不錯，這很好，因為我希望這件事能定案，

Content: and just move on to working on the game and not worry about this.
Content_zh: 然後繼續做遊戲，不要再擔心這個。

Content: But some press people took that out of our backer forums and put it up.
Content_zh: 但有些媒體把它從贊助者論壇拿出去發了。

Content: Some of them put the entire post up, which is actually better than what others did, which is take a couple words here and there
Content_zh: 有些人把整篇貼文都貼出去，這其實比其他人的做法好；其他人只截幾個字，

Content: and then put the headline as like "Kickstarter Out of Money".
Content_zh: 然後下標像是「Kickstarter 沒錢了」。

Content: You know, like implying the whole things crashing and burning.
Content_zh: 你知道，像是在暗示整件事要崩盤燒毀了。

Content: Bailey: Just eight news sources right now.
Content_zh: Bailey：目前只有八個新聞來源。

Content: Looks like it's got the Escapist.
Content_zh: 看起來有 The Escapist。

Content: Double Fine wasn't being greedy with second Kickstarter.
Content_zh: 「Double Fine 並不是因為第二個 Kickstarter 而貪婪。」

Content: I mean, there's a couple people that are just reacting like, oh crap, there goes, there goes Kickstarter.
Content_zh: 我是說，有些人反應就是，喔糟了， Kickstarter 完蛋了。

Content: It's all a sham to begin with.
Content_zh: 從一開始就是騙局。

Content: And other people are like, c'mon, right.
Content_zh: 其他人則是，拜託，對吧。

Content: Schafer: They're just, there's this whole crowd of people who are dying for anti-Kickstarter news.
Content_zh: Schafer：就是有一整群人迫不及待想看到反 Kickstarter 的新聞。

Content: And I don't know why that is, but some people are just like, Argh, I hate this Kickstarter thing.
Content_zh: 我不知道為什麼 但有些人覺得 我討厭 Kickstarter

Content: I can't wait for someone to just totally fuck it up.
Content_zh: 我等不及看有人把它徹底搞砸。

Content: That's frustrating, but what are you going to do?
Content_zh: 真讓人沮喪，但你打算怎麼辦？

Content: Just to make the game awesome, get it out there and then track them all down individually and kill them.
Content_zh: 就只能把遊戲做得超棒，推出去，然後把他們一個個找出來殺掉。

Content: Bailey: There's some cynicism out there.
Content_zh: Bailey：外面是有一些犬儒情緒。

Content: I mean, there's skepticism and that's somewhat to be expected, right?
Content_zh: 我是說，有懷疑是可以預期的，對吧？

Content: Because there hasn't been a big Kickstarter yet that's actually delivered.
Content_zh: 因為還沒有哪個大型 Kickstarter 專案真正交付成果。

Content: So I mean, I can't blame too many people for being skeptical.
Content_zh: 所以我不能怪太多人懷疑

Content: I think this all, you know, in the fullness of time will play itself out, so when people see the end product it'll, you know, people will forget about this.
Content_zh: 我想這一切最後都會自己展開；等大家看到成品，就會忘了這件事。

Content: But right now there's, yeah, there's a bit of a shit storm.
Content_zh: 但現在，對，是有點糞坑風暴。

Content: Rice: Yeah, do I look tired?
Content_zh: Rice：是啊，我看起來很累嗎？

Content: Rice: Longer than I should have been. Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：比我本該長得多是啊。

Content: Remember how after the trailer we talked about how I looked at all those forums and everything was super-positive?
Content_zh: 還記得預告片之後，我們聊到我看了那些論壇，一切都超正面嗎？

Content: It was definitely not the case last night.
Content_zh: 昨晚肯定不是

Content: So yeah, I spent a lot of time reading all of those comments and trying to see what were actual points we should think about
Content_zh: 所以，我花了很多時間來讀那些評論 試著看看我們該想點什麼

Content: and what is just kind of misinformed.
Content_zh: 哪些只是被誤導。

Content: But yeah, I don't know.
Content_zh: 但我不知道

Content: I mean, I think it's, our backers have been great about coming to our support and clarifying when people are giving kind of the wrong information
Content_zh: 我是說，我覺得我們的贊助者很棒。，他們會站出來支持我們，當有人散播錯誤資訊時幫忙澄清。

Content: and things like that. Yeah.
Content_zh: 諸如此類的事情。是啊。

Content: Schafer: We were basically all caught off guard with it leaking and then going negative so fast.
Content_zh: Schafer：這件事外流後又這麼快變負面，我們基本上都措手不及。

Content: 'Cause it's like, 'cause the more I look at it, the more you're like OK, we're taking, we're making a bigger game than we Kickstarted.
Content_zh: 因為我越看越覺得，好吧，我們是在做一款比 Kickstarter 原本承諾更大的遊戲。

Content: We are, we're delivering it later but we're paying with our own money for it and then doing this early access to fund the rest of it.
Content_zh: 我們確實比較晚交付，但我們是用自己的錢在補，然後用 Early Access 來資助剩下的部分。

Content: And then to have that framed as a total scam and a rip-off by us, just is kind of mind boggling.
Content_zh: 結果被塑造成我們在搞全面詐騙、敲竹槓，真的有點不可思議。

Content: Bailey: So basically the press leaked our stuff, which is a violation of our terms of use.
Content_zh: Bailey：所以基本上是媒體外流了我們的東西，違反了我們的使用條款。

Content: Remo: Because this is the thing when you're a journalist, like I've been a journalist
Content_zh: Remo：因為這就是你當記者的時候 就像我當記者一樣

Content: and I feel like it gives me a decent perspective on this stuff.
Content_zh: 我覺得這能讓我對這些事有好處

Content: Because as soon as one person goes live with it, every single other person who publishes it is not violating shit.
Content_zh: 因為只要有一個人先發出去，後面每個報導的人就都不算違反什麼鬼了。

Content: In fact, they're almost derelict in their duties for just ignoring it.
Content_zh: 他們因為不理會而幾乎失職了

Content: Because as far as they're, from their perspective, it's public information that's already out there.
Content_zh: 因為從他們的觀點來看 已經是公共資訊了

Content: So you're being actually just kind of a weird like lackey to some developer if you're just saying, well, they wanted me to not do it.
Content_zh: 如果你只是說，好吧，他們希望我不要報，那你反而像是某個開發商奇怪的走狗。

Content: Rice: That was the case.
Content_zh: Rice：就是這樣

Content: Like once one person broke it, then now it's public.
Content_zh: 只要有一個人爆出來，現在它就是公開資訊了。

Content: Schafer: Then looking back on it, we could have done a lot of things different, but I don't want to learn the lesson.
Content_zh: Schafer：回頭看，我們本來可以有很多不同做法，但我不想學到那個教訓。

Content: The most obvious lesson that you could learn from that, is that you should be less transparent.
Content_zh: 你從中可以學到的最明顯的教訓是 你應該少點透明度

Content: Like OK, we were just so open and transparent and all that, that we could have probably like,
Content_zh: 像是，好，我們太開放、太透明了，所以我們也許本來可以，

Content: we could have probably massaged that message better and hid a lot of stuff, but I don't know if that's really the lesson
Content_zh: 把訊息包裝得更好，藏起很多東西，但我不知道這是不是我真的想學到的教訓。

Content: I want to learn from that, you know what I mean?
Content_zh: 我想從中學習，你知道我的意思嗎？

Content: Like if we had hid the whole thing and just been like, you know, every three months push the launch date out three months
Content_zh: 如果我們把整件事藏起來，只是像大多數遊戲公司那樣，

Content: like most companies do with games, so they just slip it out.
Content_zh: 每三個月把發行日往後推三個月，慢慢滑出去。

Content: And then, then hey!
Content_zh: 然後，然後，然後嘿！

Content: By the way, I know it's not coming out 'til May, but here's an early access thing in January.
Content_zh: 順帶一提，我知道它要到五月才出，但一月有 Early Access。

Content: That's a great thing, everyone would have been wow, that's great.
Content_zh: 那就會變成很棒。的事，大家都會覺得，哇，太好了。

Content: So being more secretive could have protected us from that, from that bad public response.
Content_zh: 所以更保密也許能保護我們，免於遭遇那種負面的公開反應。

Content: But that goes against everything that we're trying to do with the project, was to be completely open and show everybody everything.
Content_zh: 但那違背了我們做這個專案的一切初衷：完全公開，讓大家看到所有事情。

Content: Remo: Part of it's also this was just like a learning experience for us in a way.
Content_zh: Remo：部分原因也是 這就像我們學習的經驗

Content: I think that even if we do have another thing that is this significant that we're putting out there,
Content_zh: 我想即使我們有另外一件重要的事情 被我們放出來

Content: I think we would just, knowing this was going to happen, probably could have done it a little smarter.
Content_zh: 我覺得我們如果知道會發生這種事，也許可以做得聰明一點。

Content: At the time, I don't know how we would have necessarily had that foresight.
Content_zh: 當時我不知道我們怎麼會有這種預感

Content: Like to the degree it would have been necessary, but I think we just need to be more careful.
Content_zh: 盡管如此 但我們需要小心一點

Content: Schafer: And now it's out there and whatever, you know, the press makes of it or whatever weird comments we get from people
Content_zh: Schafer：現在它就在外面 不管媒體怎麼說 或是我們從別人那裡得到的奇怪評論

Content: who weren't backers, that's over.
Content_zh: 不是贊助者的人那些話，就到此為止了。

Content: Well, it might go on tonight and tomorrow, but we production-wise have moved past that and are just now focusing on the game.
Content_zh: 好吧，今晚和明天可能還會繼續，但製作上我們已經跨過去了，現在就是專注做遊戲。

Content: Schafer: Hello.
Content_zh: Schafer：你好

Content: Schafer: Oh man, look at that.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，天啊，看看那。

Content: It's like the whole town.
Content_zh: 就像整座城市

Content: The whole town looks like it's got sugar, like little sugar canisters; that's awesome.
Content_zh: 整個鎮看起來像灑滿糖，像一個個小糖罐，太棒了。

Content: Gardner: Right now we're kind of like in the middle of switching from, I mean I'm sure you've heard from the spaceship to the Vella's
Content_zh: Gardner：現在我們差不多正在從太空船切換到 Vella 那邊，

Content: like neighborhood and baker town and all that stuff.
Content_zh: 像是她的鄰里、麵包小鎮那些東西。

Content: Roberts: Yeah, this is the first shot that they'll see.
Content_zh: Roberts：對，這是他們會看到的第一個鏡頭。

Content: And so what we're doing is layouts right now, where we're just basically getting the staging with the art backgrounds
Content_zh: 所以我們現在做的是 layout，基本上就是把美術背景和角色的調度先擺進去，

Content: and the characters in and we're doing what we call rough blocking.
Content_zh: 也就是我們稱為 rough blocking 的東西。

Content: So in the play-throughs we can all watch it with Tim and everybody and just say, OK, does that work?
Content_zh: 這樣在完整遊玩時，我們就能和 Tim 還有大家一起看，然後說，好，這樣行嗎？

Content: Does this work with the game?
Content_zh: 這對遊戲有用嗎？

Content: Schafer: It's pretty important, I guess, that we figure out where everyone's standing.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想我們必須找出大家的立場

Content: Because this is like the, like the final resting place for everyone, do you think?
Content_zh: 因為這像是大家最後會停留的位置，你覺得呢？

Content: Where we want them?
Content_zh: 我們要他們去哪兒？

Content: Petty: The witch is facing the wrong way.
Content_zh: Petty：女巫面向錯了。

Content: She should be facing towards the cake, but we needed enough space for you to be able to walk around without accidentally clicking on someone.
Content_zh: 她應該正對著蛋糕 但我們需要足夠的空間 讓你可以不意外地點擊某人

Content: And that was, that was where we wound up.
Content_zh: 結果位置就變成那樣。

Content: Schafer: He could also be shoved facing left over here more.
Content_zh: Schafer：他也可能被推到左邊

Content: 'Cause this thing is, this is a kind of unnecessary thing...
Content_zh: 因為這事情是，這是一種不必要的事情……

Content: Petty: We can just paint that out.
Content_zh: Petty：我們可以直接把那塊修掉。

Content: Schafer: And it looks so prominent, that you kind of want to play with it, but you could use that shelf space, too, if he was like closer to it or something?
Content_zh: Schafer：它看起來很突出，所以你想玩它，但你也可以使用那個架子的空間，如果他像更接近它什麼的？

Content: So you wouldn't be tempted to walk, like you'd be tempted to walk this way around him instead of this way.
Content_zh: 所以你不會想走 就像你想走在他身邊而不是這樣

Content: Petty: We could always put like little photos of, maybe there's like little black and white photos of him
Content_zh: Petty：我們總是可以把像小照片，也許有像小黑白照片的他

Content: as a young warrior or something.
Content_zh: 是個年輕的戰士

Content: Like he's staring at like his past, you know, or whatever.
Content_zh: 就像他像往事一樣盯著看

Content: If you want to put some little subtle stuff there.
Content_zh: 如果你想放點微妙的東西

Content: Schafer: OK.
Content_zh: Schafer：好

Content: Offscreen Voice: Well, you know, we try to put as much in as we can, because we're so limited on time.
Content_zh: 畫外音：嗯，你知道，我們盡量能放多少就放多少，因為時間非常有限。

Content: So we know a lot of this stuff is going to change once we get final dialogue, because we don't have the final actors.
Content_zh: 所以我們知道，等拿到最終對白後，很多東西都會改，因為我們還沒有正式演員。

Content: We don't have those recordings and that can change a lot of the timing.
Content_zh: 我們沒有那些錄音，而那會大幅改變很多時間點。

Content: Garner: Famous people, like real actors?
Content_zh: Garner：名人，像真正的演員？

Content: They all read dialogue a lot slower than our scratch dialogue.
Content_zh: 他們念對白都比我們的暫錄對白慢很多。

Content: Our scratch dialogue is when we go in and do recordings and we all sort of act out the characters.
Content_zh: 暫錄對白就是我們自己進去錄音，大家大致把角色演出來。

Content: Garner: Bring me a star chart and the fabric of time and space shall be knit to serve your passage.
Content_zh: Garner：把星圖拿來，時空的織物將被編織起來，服務你的旅程。

Content: [Inaudible 00:16:19] Stamos: I came in this morning and Cam's like,
Content_zh: [聽不清 00:16:19] Stamos：我今天早上進來，Cam 就說，

Content: he said we need some more mom voice.
Content_zh: 他說我們需要更多媽媽的聲音

Content: So I'm like, oh, all right.
Content_zh: 所以我喜歡，哦，好吧。

Content: I guess that's me.
Content_zh: 我猜是我

Content: Stoddard: I actually like, I really like, I like how you did the mom.
Content_zh: Stoddard：我其實很喜歡，我真的很喜歡你演媽媽的方式。

Content: Stamos: Well, I'm reading it just as me, so since I am a mom, I guess that's OK.
Content_zh: Stamos：嗯，我就是照自己的樣子念，既然我是媽媽，我想這樣也可以。

Content: Stoddard: You got a good mom voice.
Content_zh: Stoddard：你媽媽的聲音很好聽

Content: Rice: Is there anybody else on this ship?
Content_zh: Rice：這艘船上還有人嗎？

Content: So you've never seen a strange wolf around?
Content_zh: 你從沒見過奇怪的狼嗎？

Content: Stoddard: Well done. Rice: All right.
Content_zh: Stoddard：幹得好 好的

Content: Team Member: So we would like to try to get as much as could into these scenes without having to change everything entirely.
Content_zh: 團隊成員：所以我們希望盡量把能放的東西放進這些場景，不要等到拿到最終對白後全部重改。

Content: Once we get that final dialogue.
Content_zh: 一旦我們得到最後對話

Content: So we're kind of doing a lot of stuff that you would do sequentially, all at once.
Content_zh: 所以我們有點是在把很多本來會依序做的事情一次做完。

Content: Schafer: And they try not to do too much, so you can tell some of them are animating more than they should there,
Content_zh: Schafer：他們也試著不要做太多，所以你看得出有些地方動畫做得比應該做的還多，

Content: because they just wanted to make a nice scene.
Content_zh: 因為他們只是想拍個好戲

Content: And soon we're going to file dialogue for all that, so we'll be able to polish that up like we couldn't before.
Content_zh: 很快我們就會有這些最終對白，所以就能把它們打磨到以前做不到的程度。

Content: Schafer: Should we just start listening to stuff.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們該開始聽東西了

Content: Brown: Yeah, we can just go down the list.
Content_zh: Brown：是的，我們可以從名單上下來

Content: Schafer: Let's do it.
Content_zh: Schafer：走吧

Content: Khris and I started working together at Lucas Arts in the '90s.
Content_zh: Khris 和我是在 90 年代於 LucasArts 開始共事的。

Content: Like she started, Brown: 1990.
Content_zh: 就像她開始的，Brown：1990年。

Content: Schafer: What was your first job title at Lucas Arts?
Content_zh: Schafer：你在 LucasArts 的第一個職稱是什麼？

Content: Brown: I was the Product Support Supervisor.
Content_zh: Brown：我是產品支援主管

Content: Schafer: Yeah, she was in Prod Sup, yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：是的，她在普洛德蘇普，是的。

Content: And then, but the first thing you did sound on for me is Full Throttle, right?
Content_zh: 然後，你第一次幫我的作品做聲音，是《Full Throttle》對吧？

Content: Brown: Uh, Schafer: Or did Tentacle, no you were in...
Content_zh: Brown：呃，Schafer：還是《Tentacle》？不，你在……

Content: Brown: Day of the Tentacle.
Content_zh: Brown：《Day of the Tentacle》。

Content: Schafer: You were in Day of the Tentacle.
Content_zh: Schafer：你參與過《Day of the Tentacle》。

Content: Brown: We said dirty things to Mark Hamell in his headphones and that's true.
Content_zh: Brown：我們對著 Mark Hamill 的耳機講髒話，這是真的。

Content: Schafer: We learned a lot.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們學了很多

Content: Brown: And we learned a lot.
Content_zh: Brown：我們學了很多

Content: Schafer: You've learned a lot since then.
Content_zh: Schafer：從那以後你學了很多

Content: Super-professional mature now.
Content_zh: 現在超專業、超成熟。

Content: Let's listen to some more voices.
Content_zh: 讓我們聽聽更多的聲音。

Content: Schafer: I had a meeting with Khris where I actually listened to the first auditions.
Content_zh: Schafer：我和 Khris 開了會 在那裡我聽了第一次試鏡

Content: So we listened to a whole bunch of auditions.
Content_zh: 所以我們聽了一堆試鏡

Content: A few of them were good, but it's just the start.
Content_zh: 有一些是好的，但這只是開始。

Content: We're going to listen to a whole lot more auditions.
Content_zh: 我們要聽更多的試鏡

Content: We have a bunch of characters to cast.
Content_zh: 我們有很多角色要演

Content: Audition Voice: You came because you are done with children's games.
Content_zh: Audition Voice：你會來，是因為你已經受夠小孩子的遊戲了。

Content: My name is Merrick and what I'm about to show you is no game.
Content_zh: 我叫 Marek，我接下來要給你看的東西可不是遊戲。

Content: Schafer: Pretty good, but I think I like the other guy.
Content_zh: Schafer：很好，但我比較喜歡另一個人。

Content: Audition Voice: David Kaufman Merrick. Schafer: Whoa.
Content_zh: Audition Voice：David Kaufman， Marek。Schafer：哇。

Content: Audition Voice: You came because you are done with children's games.
Content_zh: Audition Voice：你會來，是因為你已經受夠小孩子的遊戲了。

Content: My name is Merrick and what I'm about to show you is no game.
Content_zh: 我叫 Marek，我接下來要給你看的東西可不是遊戲。

Content: While you've been drifting through the universe, eating ice cream and playing with trains, war has been raging across the galaxy.
Content_zh: 當你漂流在宇宙中 吃著冰淇淋 玩著火車時 戰爭一直在星系中肆虐

Content: Between the forces that every war is between, between the powerful and the weak, the tyrants and the innocent.
Content_zh: 在每場戰爭的勢力之間 權力和弱勢之間 暴君和無辜者之間

Content: Does it even matter there will always be war.
Content_zh: 這重要嗎？戰爭永遠都會存在。

Content: We did not start it and we cannot stop it.
Content_zh: 不是我們挑起的，我們也無法阻止。

Content: But what we can do is protect the weak and rescue the helpless.
Content_zh: 但是我們能做的是保護弱者 拯救無助者

Content: Schafer: That guy's pretty awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：那傢伙真棒。

Content: Is it a higher pitch than I imagined him having?
Content_zh: 比我想像的要高？

Content: But I love how he read it.
Content_zh: 但我喜歡他的念法。

Content: Schafer: Well, we had our first casting session.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，我們剛進行了第一次選角會議。

Content: Khris was on Skype and I was listening to all the auditions.
Content_zh: Khris 在 Skype 上，我在聽所有試鏡。

Content: They were good.
Content_zh: 很棒。

Content: There were a couple characters that I felt like, that's exactly right; we could use that voice.
Content_zh: 有幾個角色我覺得，對，就是這樣；我們可以用那個聲音。

Content: For a lot of them, and the main ones, I think it's going to be a longer process where we just take the first listen
Content_zh: 對很多角色，尤其主要角色，我覺得會是更長的過程，我們只是先聽第一輪。

Content: and then Khris and I have something that we can talk to together.
Content_zh: 然後 Khris 和我就有東西可以一起討論。

Content: Like where is this, instead of just reaching out in the dark, we're like, OK, now we've heard a voice.
Content_zh: 而不是在黑暗中摸索；我們現在會說，好，我們已經聽到一個聲音了。

Content: Is it older, younger than this?
Content_zh: 這聲音要再老一點，還是再年輕一點？

Content: Is it meaner or nicer than this?
Content_zh: 比這更兇，還是更親切？

Content: You know, we can talk about all the different ways we want to use that as a landmark to know, navigate to actual voice that we want.
Content_zh: 我們可以把它當成地標，討論各種方向，慢慢導航到我們真正想要的聲音。

Content: Is that a good metaphor?
Content_zh: 這是個好比喻嗎？

Content: Schafer: OK. Well, we have a company meeting at 2:00 o'clock also.
Content_zh: Schafer：好 我們兩點還有公司會議

Content: Brown: Oh.
Content_zh: Brown：喔。

Content: Schafer: Oh God. What do you think is the most important thing to listen to?
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊 你認為最重要的事情是什麼？

Content: Brown: I think the most important things to listen to are Shay and Vella.
Content_zh: Brown：我想最重要的是 Shay 和 Vella

Content: Schafer: Vella.
Content_zh: Schafer：Vella。

Content: Let's listen to some Vellas.
Content_zh: 來聽幾個 Vella 吧。

Content: Audition Voice: Need help cleaning up?
Content_zh: Audition Voice：需要幫忙打掃嗎？

Content: Schafer: There's something wrong with that one.
Content_zh: Schafer：那個有點不對勁。

Content: I don't know what it is.
Content_zh: 我不知道是哪裡。

Content: Audition Voice: Hi. Need help cleaning up?
Content_zh: Audition Voice：嗨 需要幫忙打掃嗎？

Content: Schafer: Not bad.
Content_zh: Schafer：不錯

Content: Audition Voice: I think it's time we start fighting that monster.
Content_zh: Audition Voice：我想我們該和那個怪物戰鬥了

Content: Schafer: It sounds really young to me, it's like her little sister, but I don't know.
Content_zh: Schafer：對我來說聽起來真的很年輕，像她妹妹，但我也不確定。

Content: I'm trying to think of what 14 year old girls sound like.
Content_zh: 我在想 14 歲女孩聽起來是什麼樣子。

Content: Khris: There's a sample of a 15 year old girl and her audition is bitchy and I don't like it,
Content_zh: Khris：有一個 15 歲女孩的樣本，她的試鏡很跩，我不喜歡，

Content: but it just gives you an idea of what a 15 year old girl sounds like.
Content_zh: 但它至少能讓你知道 15 歲女孩聽起來是什麼樣子。

Content: Audition Voice: What you got?
Content_zh: Audition Voice：你有什麼？

Content: Let me see that.
Content_zh: 讓我看看

Content: Nah, I don't really need it that bad.
Content_zh: 不，我真的不需要它。

Content: Schafer: Her acting is kind of a problem.
Content_zh: Schafer：她的演技有點問題

Content: Hm. OK. All right.
Content_zh: 嗯，好，好吧

Content: Brown: Oh no! I hate to make you sad.
Content_zh: Brown：喔，不！我不想讓你傷心

Content: Do you want to hear all the "B" list ones that didn't get put up?
Content_zh: 你想聽那些沒有上傳的 B 級名單嗎？

Content: Schafer: Oh no, I was just saying, you know, I mean listen to them all.
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，不，我只是說，你知道，我的意思是聽他們全部。

Content: Brown: We have tons of auditions.
Content_zh: Brown：我們有很多次試鏡

Content: Schafer: I know. I mean, is there anything really unique in there?
Content_zh: Schafer：我知道 裡面有什麼特別的嗎？

Content: I guess I was looking for something that was kind of special, unique in some way.
Content_zh: 我想我在找一些特殊，獨特的東西

Content: Brown: Special like?
Content_zh: Brown：特別嗎？

Content: Schafer: Super likable. They're all good.
Content_zh: Schafer：超棒的 他們都很好

Content: I mean, they're all good.
Content_zh: 我的意思是，他們都很好。

Content: They're all in the right, correct range, I think, in age for the most part, except for that one that sounded really young.
Content_zh: 他們都在正確的範圍，我想，在大部份的年齡，除了那個聽起來很年輕。

Content: I guess I was looking for one with like a personality that would leap out at me.
Content_zh: 我想我在找一個像個性會跳出來的人

Content: But I think it just takes time.
Content_zh: 但我覺得這需要時間

Content: I think I just have to listen to them a whole bunch of times.
Content_zh: 我想我只是得多聽幾次。

Content: Brown: Yeah, but I don't want you to be like beat into submission, beaten into submission,
Content_zh: Brown：對，但我不希望你像是被逼到屈服，

Content: like and reconcile yourself to something less than what you truly want, you know?
Content_zh: 然後勉強接受不是真正想要的東西，你懂嗎？

Content: Schafer: When would I ever do that?
Content_zh: Schafer：我什麼時候會這麼做？

Content: When would I ever let that happen?
Content_zh: 我什麼時候能讓這種事發生？

Content: Anyway, that's how it goes.
Content_zh: 總之，事情就是這樣的

Content: Schafer: You get the first listen and now Khris and I, whoa, [inaudible 00:22:08], I spit all over my Rubik's cubes.
Content_zh: Schafer：第一次聽完之後，現在 Khris 和我，哇，[聽不清 00:22:08]，我口水噴滿了我的魔術方塊。

Content: Yeah, no, she has a little clearer idea of what I think of each character now that we have something to compare it to.
Content_zh: 對，不，她對我對每個角色的看法有更清楚的瞭解 因為我們有東西可以比對

Content: And I'm still, still pursuing some like, friends, friends and famous people for the game.
Content_zh: 而且我還在替這款遊戲找一些朋友、朋友和名人。

Content: We'll see where that goes.
Content_zh: 看看會怎樣

Content: I don't want to say anything , 'cause what if it doesn't happen, I'll just feel stupid.
Content_zh: 我不想說，因為萬一沒成，我會覺得自己很蠢。

Content: Schafer: Possible, one might say celebrity cameos in the game?
Content_zh: Schafer：可以說遊戲裡可能會有名人客串嗎？

Content: Me, just me.
Content_zh: 我啦，只有我。

Content: Actual bona fide celebrities.
Content_zh: 真正貨真價實的名人。

Content: Offscreen Voice: People that we've used, actors that we've used before?
Content_zh: 畫外音：我們以前合作過的人、以前用過的演員嗎？

Content: Schafer: My hope is to use actually a lot of people that we have used before, just for some fun small parts,
Content_zh: Schafer：我希望其實能用很多以前合作過的人，演一些有趣的小角色，

Content: like Richard Horovitz and Nicky Rapp and contact Mr. Black, see if he's available.
Content_zh: 比如理查德·霍羅維茨和尼克·拉普 聯繫布萊克先生 看看他是否有空

Content: He offered to do that, like before.
Content_zh: 他以前提過願意做。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Oh, he did?
Content_zh: 畫外音：喔，他有嗎？

Content: Schafer: He said, Offscreen Voice: He should be the hipster lumberjack.
Content_zh: Schafer：他說…… 畫外音：他應該演潮流伐木工。

Content: Petty: You know, last week I got some of the UI stuff cut up and integrated.
Content_zh: Petty：你知道，上週我把一些 UI 東西切好並整合進去了。

Content: And I also got most of the way through doing the kind of like, they're not implemented in the game yet, but the white box for all the Shellmound.
Content_zh: 我也大致完成了所有 Shellmound 的 white box；它們還沒實作進遊戲裡。

Content: My goal is to get those all in the game and hooked up by end of the week.
Content_zh: 我的目標是在這週結束前，把它們全放進遊戲並接好。

Content: Petty: This is really kind of where a white box, now we do have some basic layering set up here.
Content_zh: Petty：這裡基本上就是 white box，不過我們現在已經有一些基本分層。

Content: And I did some basic camera work.
Content_zh: 我也做了一些基本的鏡頭處理。

Content: But this shows the basic layout of the scene, the key elements in place.
Content_zh: 但這顯示了場景的基本 layout，關鍵元素到位。

Content: So these are, you know, just quick black and white drawings, but they, some of them like this, without going into too much detail,
Content_zh: 所以這些是，你知道的，只是快速的黑白畫，但他們，有些像這樣，沒有太多的細節，

Content: this particular scene has a very strange layout in some ways because you have to see this sort of distant view of Shellmound,
Content_zh: 這個特定場景的 layout 在某些方面很奇怪，因為你必須看到 Shellmound 的遠景，

Content: because it sets up something that happens later.
Content_zh: 因為它安排了一些後來發生的事

Content: So it really wasn't that any of these are the final shapes or languages or anything.
Content_zh: 所以這些都不是最終的形狀、視覺語言或任何定稿。

Content: Or even really the final shot, as much to make sure that all those elements had a place and could work together in a single screen composition.
Content_zh: 甚至也不算最終鏡頭，重點是確保所有元素都有位置，能在單一畫面構圖中一起運作。

Content: So you kind of have to work at a certain level of detail just to be able to figure that out and to communicate that to Bagel.
Content_zh: 所以你得做到某種細節程度，才能弄清楚並把它傳達給 Bagel。

Content: Petty: My goal is to get all those all in the game and hooked by end of the week.
Content_zh: Petty：我的目標是在這週結束前，把這些全放進遊戲並接好。

Content: Schafer: It will close, too.
Content_zh: Schafer：也快接近了。

Content: Being almost all white boxed.
Content_zh: 幾乎全都 white box 完了。

Content: Camden, world of sound.
Content_zh: Camden，音效世界。

Content: Stoddard: Last week it was mostly filling out space ship and there's still a few glitches timing stuff that I have to work on and kind of tighten up.
Content_zh: Stoddard：上週主要是在補太空船，還有一些 timing 上的小問題得處理、收緊。

Content: Stoddard: Yeah.
Content_zh: Stoddard：對。

Content: It's a super high tech spaceship, but at the same time, like I kind of think it would be kind of fun to like have stuff kind of squeaky
Content_zh: 這是超高科技太空船，但同時我覺得讓一些東西有點吱嘎作響會很有趣。

Content: and you can hear the wheels and the joints working and things like that.
Content_zh: 你可以聽到輪子和關節的聲音 以及那樣的聲音

Content: So I'm trying to get some elements with Laura's key card.
Content_zh: 所以我想用 Laura 的門禁卡收一些元素。

Content: Laura: Ready? Stoddard: Yep.
Content_zh: Laura：好了嗎？是的

Content: Great. Awesome. Laura: Yeah, yeah? Stoddard: Perfect.
Content_zh: 太好了。很棒。。Laura：可以嗎？Stoddard：完美。

Content: And I'm starting to do some things with like phase and modulation.
Content_zh: 我也開始做一些像 phase 和 modulation 的東西。

Content: [Squeaky sounds].
Content_zh: [吱嘎聲]。

Content: Getting like this almost like alien machinery sound.
Content_zh: 就像是外星機器一樣

Content: But I was also noticing some of its properties I could do for like some of the robots.
Content_zh: 但我也注意到它的一些屬性 我可以做一些機器人。

Content: Like almost chattering.
Content_zh: 就像在聊天

Content: Schafer: Cool. Rusty?
Content_zh: Schafer：酷。Rusty？

Content: Russell: Beginning of the week was mostly some prop stuff for the girl's house.
Content_zh: Russell：這周開始 主要是給女孩家的道具

Content: And then it's up to the Mog Chothra one so you get the textures done.
Content_zh: 接下來輪到 Mog Chothra，所以你要把材質做完。

Content: Russell: So Adrien was actually involved with doing the character modeling and the rigging, but he went off to another project to help out.
Content_zh: Russell：Adrien 其實有參與角色建模和 rigging，但他去另一個專案幫忙了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: This was supposed to be a shot of you, hard at work on another project.
Content_zh: 畫外音：這本來應該是拍你在另一個專案努力工作的畫面。

Content: [Laughter] Skillman: The backers are going to love that.
Content_zh: [笑聲] Skillman：贊助者一定會喜歡。

Content: Russell: But because they had a vacancy to fill because Adrien left, I'm the next guy up.
Content_zh: Russell：但因為 Adrien 離開後有空缺要補，我就是下一個頂上的人。

Content: So yeah.
Content_zh: 所以是的。

Content: Some of the stuff I'm doing is just like going back through old files.
Content_zh: 我做的一些事情就像翻過舊檔案一樣

Content: Like a lot of this stuff was made for the actual teaser trailer.
Content_zh: 很多東西其實是為原本的 teaser trailer 做的。

Content: And Adrien's got a lot of rigging things that he did for us that in the meantime that some of these older rigs need to get updated with.
Content_zh: Adrien 之前幫我們做了很多 rigging，現在有些舊 rig 得拿那些東西來更新。

Content: So like this girl, this is the girl in the cake that you probably saw in the trailer.
Content_zh: 像這個女孩，這就是你可能在預告片裡看到、在蛋糕裡的女孩。

Content: I'm adding actually the lower half of her body so that when she gets pulled out, you know, she actually has a body that comes with it.
Content_zh: 我加入她的下半身 所以當她被拉出來時 她的身體會隨之而來

Content: So he was also done pretty early on in the project, so we kind of revamped him and revamped his skeleton.
Content_zh: 所以他也是專案很早期就做好的，我們就重新改造他，也改了他的骨架。

Content: So he's got some, some other controls now that you can kind of do a little parallax so that if he's trying to turn, this part can,
Content_zh: 所以他現在有一些其他控制，可以做一點 parallax；如果他要轉身，這一塊就可以，

Content: you know, look like it's going behind him a little bit.
Content_zh: 看起來好像有點落後了

Content: He's got the ability to open his jaw and stuff now.
Content_zh: 他現在有能力打開他的下巴和東西

Content: Schafer: Jessie! Whoa! That's scary.
Content_zh: Schafer：傑西！哇！真可怕

Content: Schafer: I've actually been able to write now so I wrote the Maidens Feast stuff as you saw
Content_zh: Schafer：我其實終於能寫東西了，所以我寫了你們看到的 Maidens Feast 內容。

Content: and that's been delivered and Malena's putting that in the database right now.
Content_zh: 那已經交付了，Malena 現在正在把它放進資料庫。

Content: And then the next thing I'm going to be working on, I think is Shellmound.
Content_zh: 接下來我想我要做的是 Shellmound。

Content: Schafer: I think I'm going to plunge ahead with it, new stuff just to make sure no one's blocked by dialogue ever again.
Content_zh: Schafer：我想我要繼續往前衝，先寫新的東西，確保再也沒有人被對白卡住。

Content: Schafer: I hope that I get the dialogue done in the next month or two.
Content_zh: Schafer：希望我能在接下來一兩個月把對白寫完。

Content: All of it done and then it'll just be me reviewing and redesigning and rewriting stuff.
Content_zh: 全部寫完，然後就只剩我審閱、重新設計和改寫。

Content: Play testing.
Content_zh: 試玩測試。

Content: Schafer: Holy crap. Bailey: I know.
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊 我知道

Content: I was playing Day of the Tentacle and I was playing some of the other old games and I just noticed that
Content_zh: 我在玩《Day of the Tentacle》和其他一些老遊戲時注意到，

Content: in a lot of those games there's multiple writers.
Content_zh: 很多那類遊戲都有多位編劇。

Content: Is there a way to bring on a junior writer?
Content_zh: 有沒有可能找一位 junior writer 進來？

Content: Schafer: I mean, I'm always open to that.
Content_zh: Schafer：我的意思是，我總是開放的。

Content: We did that on Psychonauts.
Content_zh: 我們在《Psychonauts》就是這樣做的。

Content: It's just always been, it's really been hard to find people that do it exactly, exactly right.
Content_zh: 一直以來 很難找到真正正確的人

Content: And it's also, it's kind of time consuming to read a bunch of writing samples.
Content_zh: 而且看一堆寫作樣本很耗時

Content: It's a possibility, like if some rock star writer just walked in the door and was ready to go, I would be totally open to that.
Content_zh: 有可能。如果某個超強編劇剛好走進門，而且準備好開工，我完全願意。

Content: This is where you cut to some rock star writer walking through the door.
Content_zh: 這裡就切到某個超強編劇走進門的畫面。

Content: Someone that actually looks like a rock star, but he's got pen and paper.
Content_zh: 看起來像搖滾明星 但他有筆和紙

Content: Petty: Would you rather have help there or in design?
Content_zh: Petty：你寧願在那裡或設計方面得到幫助嗎？

Content: Schafer: Well, design I can always get help by just having brainstorming sessions with people and having people,
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，設計方面我總是可以靠和大家開腦力激盪會、找人聊來得到幫助，

Content: you know, to talk to and to give ideas.
Content_zh: 你知道，談話和提供想法。

Content: Everybody's got like a lot of ideas to offer.
Content_zh: 每個人都有很多想法

Content: Petty: We had the ax, which I presume he could just hand you.
Content_zh: Petty：我們有斧頭 我想他可以把斧頭交給你

Content: And he makes a stool, you could make here.
Content_zh: 他做了一張凳子，你可以在這裡做。

Content: There's a bathroom where you get the stool.
Content_zh: 浴室裡也有一張凳子可以拿。

Content: That'll be the only part of this meeting that Paul puts on the next episode.
Content_zh: 這會是 Paul 放進下一集的這場會議唯一片段。

Content: Rice: Well, I mean, how are feeling?
Content_zh: Rice：感覺如何？

Content: Schafer: I think what I want to do is put all those cuts in and really just do a puzzle graph,
Content_zh: Schafer：我想做的是把所有那些 cut 放進去，然後真的做一張謎題圖，

Content: where I'm looking at all the puzzles that are in the game, and counting them and see how many we have,
Content_zh: 看遊戲裡所有謎題，把它們算出來，看我們有多少，

Content: how that compares to past games and kind of hours of game play.
Content_zh: 再和過去遊戲以及大概遊玩時數相比。

Content: 'Cause I don't want to have a game that's like, you know, three hours long.
Content_zh: 因為我不想做出一款只有三小時長的遊戲。

Content: I just want to make sure that we're still having that puzzle count...
Content_zh: 我只是想確保我們還有那個謎題數量……

Content: Rice: You see, that's funny to me, because it seems like you're always complaining about long games.
Content_zh: Rice：你看，這對我來說很好笑，因為你好像老是在抱怨遊戲太長。

Content: It seems like everyone in the office enjoys short experiences.
Content_zh: 辦公室裡的每個人似乎都喜歡短暫的經驗

Content: Schafer: Well, there's short and then there's like, holy crap, I can't believe they took two years to make this game.
Content_zh: Schafer：嗯，短是一回事，短到讓人覺得，天啊，我不敢相信他們花兩年做出這遊戲，又是另一回事。

Content: Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Rice：對。

Content: But I mean, the longer you go, the just like more dilute it gets, right?
Content_zh: 但我的意思是，時間越拉長，就越被稀釋，對吧？

Content: And just in general it seems like favoring a well-polished core experience over, Schafer: I'm not talking about those kind of things.
Content_zh: 整體來說，比起……似乎更應該偏向一個打磨好的核心體驗。Schafer：我不是在講那種事。

Content: I'm not talking about diluting it.
Content_zh: 我不是說稀釋它

Content: I'm saying like, like we, Rice: But it's what we're doing, right?
Content_zh: 我是說，像我們…… Rice：但這就是我們正在做的事，對吧？

Content: Schafer: No, i'm not, hold on- Rice: There's only a certain amount, Schafer: Shh.
Content_zh: Schafer：不，我不是，等一下。Rice：數量就是有限。Schafer：噓。

Content: Let me get this out.
Content_zh: 讓我把話說完。

Content: Like Lee and I were talking, like in Stacking.
Content_zh: 像 Lee 和我以前談《Stacking》的時候。

Content: We talked about Stacking.
Content_zh: 我們談過《Stacking》。

Content: The content of stacking is not low, like there's tons of density and tons of characters and stuff like that.
Content_zh: 《Stacking》的內容量並不低，裡面密度很高、角色也很多。

Content: And we used to talk about ways of it trying to extend, like there's content people just miss when they go through it,
Content_zh: 我們以前會討論怎麼延伸它，像是有些內容玩家一路玩過去會錯過，

Content: that possibly mechanically we could have brought more into the critical path of the game, right?
Content_zh: 也許從機制上，我們可以把更多東西拉進遊戲的關鍵路徑，對吧？

Content: And it always be, no matter what we do when we ship, there'll be a big call, like it's too short
Content_zh: 而且不管我們推出時做了什麼，一定都會有人大聲說太短了。

Content: because people always complain about it especially adventure games being too short because they rush through them.
Content_zh: 因為大家總是在抱怨，尤其冒險遊戲太短，因為他們一路衝過去。

Content: You know, some people want to rush through them, and then they're like, ah, it's too short.
Content_zh: 你知道，有些人就是想衝完，然後又說，啊，太短了。

Content: Rice: I do think it's a recent phenomenon, though.
Content_zh: Rice：但我覺得這是最近的事

Content: People arguing the other way.
Content_zh: 人們在爭吵

Content: Schafer: We're not going to be able to make this game too long.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們不可能把這遊戲做得太長。

Content: There's nothing we can do to make this game feel too long or be too long, because you just cannot extend that content, that's hand-made in that way.
Content_zh: 我們沒有任何辦法讓這遊戲感覺太長或真的太長，因為那種手工內容根本沒辦法這樣延展。

Content: We have no systems that we could accidentally turn the screws on and all of a sudden make it.
Content_zh: 我們沒有哪個系統可以不小心把強度轉高，突然就變長。

Content: Rice: Yeah, I guess I'm just, don't see it as an issue if it feels short.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我想我只是覺得，如果它感覺短，這不算問題。

Content: Schafer: I do, but I do.
Content_zh: Schafer：我願意，但我願意

Content: I think there's a certain value proposition.
Content_zh: 我覺得有某種價值主張在。

Content: Like a ripped off feeling if it's not, you know, there's definitely a minimum.
Content_zh: 如果沒有達到，你知道，確實會有一種被坑的感覺，肯定有個最低限度。

Content: Rice: We only have so much money to spend and so much time on this game, so like, the more content we put in, the less time we have to polish it.
Content_zh: Rice：我們能花在這遊戲上的錢和時間就這麼多，所以內容放得越多，能打磨的時間就越少。

Content: So it's a delicate balance.
Content_zh: 所以這是很微妙的平衡。

Content: But I think he's just, he just doesn't want us to like finish and we do a full play-test of the game and it's like two hours long.
Content_zh: 但我覺得他只是，他只是不希望我們做完後完整試玩一次，結果遊戲只有兩小時。

Content: And it just feels empty, but like, there's no way that's going to happen.
Content_zh: 而且感覺很空；但那絕對不可能發生。

Content: Like I'm, I'm pretty sure it's going to be like 8, 10 hours, just based on what we've seen.
Content_zh: 就我看過的東西來說，我很確定它會有八到十小時。

Content: Rice: Yeah, and I think Full Throttle's the one specifically people always call out to him,
Content_zh: Rice：對，而且我覺得大家特別常對他提起的是《Full Throttle》。

Content: and I think it is, 'cause it is a more like linear narrative kind of, like I don't feel like I got stuck a ton in Full Throttle.
Content_zh: 我覺得是，因為它比較像線性敘事；我不覺得我在《Full Throttle》卡關很多。

Content: And I don't think anyone who'd play it now would say it's short.
Content_zh: 我覺得沒人會說它很短

Content: Rice: We are making an 8, 10 hour game maybe, who knows?
Content_zh: Rice：我們也許正在做一款八到十小時的遊戲，誰知道？

Content: But like either way, we're making an hour of music so it's not going to fill the whole game.
Content_zh: 但不管怎樣，我們只做一小時音樂，所以不可能填滿整款遊戲。

Content: So does that mean we have parts that don't have music?
Content_zh: 那意味著我們有部分沒有音樂嗎？

Content: That rely on ambience?
Content_zh: 那表示有些部分要靠環境音嗎？

Content: Does that mean we write extra music like we did with Cloud Colony?
Content_zh: 那表示我們要像 Cloud Colony 那樣多寫一些音樂嗎？

Content: But it seems like there's conflicting opinions between people on the team.
Content_zh: 但團隊裡大家的意見似乎有衝突。

Content: Schafer: Between Brian and Peter? Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：布萊恩和彼得之間？是的

Content: Min: It's just that things like we have the Cloud Colony, for example, where we made changes, McConnell: Right.
Content_zh: Min：只是像 Cloud Colony 這種地方，我們做了修改。McConnell：對。

Content: Min: so like, you know, a month goes by, we have to keep moving.
Content_zh: Min：所以，你知道，一個月過去，我們必須繼續前進。

Content: But you know, we couldn't get feedback from you so there's inefficiencies there so, McConnell: I think, but I didn't know there was feedback to give.
Content_zh: 但你知道，我們拿不到你的回饋，所以就有效率不佳的地方。McConnell：我想，但我不知道有回饋要給。

Content: I think we, I do think we need to revisit the Cloud Colony stuff because quite honestly, the new version doesn't work for me.
Content_zh: 我確實覺得我們得重新看 Cloud Colony 的東西，因為老實說，新版本對我來說不成立。

Content: It feels too fast.
Content_zh: 感覺太快了

Content: It doesn't feel drifty and floaty anymore.
Content_zh: 不再有漂浮的感覺

Content: And you know, I ran it by Tim and he felt the same way.
Content_zh: 你知道，我拿給 Tim 看過，他也有同樣感覺。

Content: Min: You have to understand where I'm coming from, I'm trying to meet deadlines to make sure that we have certain things, certain momentum.
Content_zh: Min：你得理解我的立場，我是在試著趕期限，確保我們有某些東西、某種進度。

Content: So the creative feedback is nothing personal, we're not trying to inject our own kind of thing.
Content_zh: 所以創意回饋不是針對個人，我們不是想塞進自己的東西。

Content: McConnell: Well, Brian, listen.
Content_zh: McConnell：布萊恩 聽著

Content: I understand totally.
Content_zh: 我完全理解

Content: You were just trying to solve a problem.
Content_zh: 你只是想解決一個問題

Content: Min: Yes. That's exactly right.
Content_zh: Min：是的 沒錯

Content: McConnell: The responsibility for not checking the game sooner, it'll never happen again.
Content_zh: McConnell：沒有更早檢查遊戲，責任在我，這不會再發生了。

Content: Stoddard: Well, clearly the workflow needs better communication from here on out, between the four of us.
Content_zh: Stoddard：很明顯，從現在開始，我們四個人的工作流程需要更好的溝通。

Content: McConnell: Yeah, totally.
Content_zh: McConnell：是的，完全。

Content: Stoddard: Pete is contracted only for a certain amount of music and we have to find a way to stretch it out,
Content_zh: Stoddard：Pete 的合約只包含一定量的音樂，我們得想辦法把它延展開來，

Content: because the game's going to need a lot more time than that.
Content_zh: 因為遊戲需要的時間會多很多。

Content: McConnell: You know, how do you cover, how do you cover all the ground that you need to cover?
Content_zh: McConnell：你知道，要怎麼涵蓋，怎麼涵蓋所有需要涵蓋的範圍？

Content: I mean, that's really what's behind this question, right? Min: Yeah.
Content_zh: 我是說，這才是問題的背後，對吧？對。

Content: Rice: So Peter, I was talking to Brian about this and I think it is actually a lot, McConnell: It's an important question!
Content_zh: Rice：所以 Peter，我有跟 Brian 談過這個，我覺得這其實是很…… McConnell：這是個重要問題！

Content: Rice: Yeah, and I think it's actually really similar to what we've been facing with the art team.
Content_zh: Rice：對，而且我覺得這其實和我們在美術團隊面對的問題很像。

Content: 'Cause you know, when we started out on this game the whole idea was that it was going to be a really tiny adventure game
Content_zh: 因為當我們開始這遊戲的時候 整個想法是 它將是一個非常小的冒險遊戲

Content: and Bagel was going to draw every character and every environment in the game.
Content_zh: Bagel 打算畫出遊戲中的每個角色和環境

Content: McConnell: Right.
Content_zh: McConnell：對

Content: Rice: And obviously it's just a much larger game now to where he can't do all of the art.
Content_zh: Rice：而且顯然現在遊戲大了很多，他不可能做完所有美術。

Content: So we've had to expand the art team and figure out how to make that work and how to make the game feel like it all looks like what Bagel would do,
Content_zh: 所以我們必須擴大美術團隊，弄清楚怎麼讓它運作，並讓遊戲感覺整體都像 Bagel 會做出的東西，

Content: even though there's been a lot of hands touching it.
Content_zh: 雖然有很多人碰過它

Content: It's going to be a matter of getting pieces from you that essentially feel like concepts for this is what Cloud Colony music sounds like
Content_zh: 重點會是從你那裡拿到一些片段，本質上像是概念：Cloud Colony 的音樂應該是這樣，

Content: and this is everywhere it goes.
Content_zh: 以及它會用在所有這些地方。

Content: And then we let Brian and Camden run with it a bit and just keep throwing stuff over to you to see if you like
Content_zh: 然後讓 Brian 和 Camden 稍微延伸發揮，並一直丟東西給你看，確認你喜不喜歡，

Content: the direction that they're kind of expanding things.
Content_zh: 他們擴展出去的方向。

Content: McConnell: I mean, I actually think it's going to be an interesting problem to solve, to you know, work with you guys to make this score bigger.
Content_zh: McConnell：我是說，我其實覺得這會是個有趣的問題，可以跟你們一起把這份配樂做得更大。

Content: So I mean, I'm looking forward to it.
Content_zh: 所以我很期待

Content: I'm not seeing this as a, you know, I just see it as what we're dealing with and something that has interesting, you know, an interesting challenge.
Content_zh: 我不是把它看成什麼問題；我只是把它看成我們正在處理的事情，一個有趣的挑戰。

Content: [Music] McConnell: Back in Boston, after I got out of college,
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：回到波士頓，在我大學畢業後，

Content: one of my housemates in Boston was Michael Land.
Content_zh: 我在波士頓的一位室友是麥可蘭德

Content: And we worked together at, and he later became the Sound Director at Lucas.
Content_zh: 我們一起工作 他後來成為盧卡斯的音效主管

Content: I saved up some money and quit my job at Lexcon and drove out West and the idea was to, you know, start a band.
Content_zh: 我存了一點錢，辭掉 Lexicon 的工作，開車往西，想法是去組樂團。

Content: Well, by the time I got here, he had gotten his job at Lucasarts and so you know, you got to check this out.
Content_zh: 嗯，等我到這裡時，他已經在 LucasArts 找到工作了，所以他說，你得來看看這個。

Content: I got involved in that and first as a consultant and then, and then, you know, I joined the Sound Department.
Content_zh: 我參與了這項工作，先是做顧問 然後，你知道，我加入了音效部

Content: And he definitely helped get me started in game music.
Content_zh: 他肯定幫我開始了遊戲音樂

Content: So I'm very grateful to him.
Content_zh: 所以我非常感激他。

Content: My biggest tool for really starting is this handheld thing here.
Content_zh: 我真正開始創作時最重要的工具，就是這個手持裝置。

Content: I just walk around and hum stuff into it.
Content_zh: 我只是走來走去 哼哼東西進去。

Content: [sings notes from Star Wars theme] If I do any more, we'll have to pay John Williams.
Content_zh: [哼唱《星際大戰》主題音符] 如果再多唱一點，我們就得付 John Williams 錢了。

Content: You know, I know how to operate this thing.
Content_zh: 我知道怎麼操作這玩意

Content: Ah, gee, that's a pretty catchy tune.
Content_zh: 啊，天啊，這曲子很吸引人

Content: Maybe I should, maybe I should use that one.
Content_zh: 也許我該用那個

Content: So I would hum into this machine.
Content_zh: 所以我會對著這台機器哼。

Content: Then come back to it and see if I still liked the melody.
Content_zh: 再來看看我是否還喜歡旋律

Content: I'm a big melody guy.
Content_zh: 我是很重視旋律的人。

Content: You really need to go home whistling something.
Content_zh: 你真的需要回家吹口哨

Content: If the person who listens to it, if they're a musician or not, they're going to sit there and hum it later.
Content_zh: 聽的人不管是不是音樂家，之後都會坐在那裡哼它。

Content: For Psychonauts, I used to send Tim, you know, little MP3 files of you know, hey, here's what I'm working on, Tim.
Content_zh: 做《Psychonauts》時，我以前會寄一些小 MP3 檔給 Tim，像是，嘿， Tim，這是我正在做的東西。

Content: You know, [Hums a theme] Great.
Content_zh: 你知道，[主題] 太好了

Content: I think I'll have to hear it more in context.
Content_zh: 我想我得在背景上多聽聽

Content: [Music] McConnell: So then the next stage would be to actually
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：所以下一個階段就是實際上，

Content: sort of work it out at the piano.
Content_zh: 在鋼琴那裡

Content: And notate it.
Content_zh: 然後把它記譜。

Content: Broken Age is a really interesting one, actually.
Content_zh: 《Broken Age》其實真的很有意思。

Content: I think it's one of the hardest scores I've ever done, because in something like Grim Fandango there are,
Content_zh: 我覺得這是我做過最難的配樂之一，因為像《Grim Fandango》那樣，

Content: there're sort of recognizable worlds with, with sort of little plums of you know, this is sort of central American city with a noire, an art deco vibe to it.
Content_zh: 裡面有某種可辨識的世界，帶有一些小線索：這是帶黑色電影、裝飾藝術氣息的中美洲城市。

Content: This is a dock, like in the Maltese Falcon.
Content_zh: 這是碼頭，像《馬爾他之鷹》那樣。

Content: There're real specific cultural references in that and Psychonauts, there're really specific cultural references.
Content_zh: 那裡有非常具體的文化參照；《Psychonauts》也有很具體的文化參照。

Content: And if there weren't, I stuck them in there.
Content_zh: 如果沒有，我把它們塞進去了

Content: But Broken Age is, it's a fable about a very, a completely imaginary world.
Content_zh: 但《Broken Age》是一則關於完全想像世界的寓言。

Content: The village, the Meriloft, the spaceship; they're these very, they're worlds unto themselves.
Content_zh: 村莊、Meriloft、太空船；它們都是非常獨立的世界。

Content: And so I'm really trying to find sounds that are also sounds unto themselves.
Content_zh: 所以我也真的在找那種自成一格的聲音。

Content: My initial feeling was that the town needed to have this sort of combination of maybe an African sort of vibe and also a Scandinavian vibe.
Content_zh: 我最初的感覺是，鎮上需要某種非洲氛圍和斯堪地那維亞氛圍的結合。

Content: One of the Scandinavian instruments that I was interested in is called a kantele, and it's a dulcimer, hammer dulcimer-like instrument.
Content_zh: 我感興趣的一種斯堪地那維亞樂器叫 kantele，是一種類似揚琴、擊弦揚琴的樂器。

Content: So I have a kantele sample here.
Content_zh: 所以我這裡有一個 kantele sample。

Content: A lot of these samples are going to get replaced by real instruments.
Content_zh: 這些 sample 有很多會被真正的樂器取代。

Content: The autoharp was another one.
Content_zh: autoharp 是另一個。

Content: [Music] McConnell: And that's the kantele.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：那就是 kantele。

Content: Cello.
Content_zh: 大提琴。

Content: Kalimba, which is a, this is a kalimba.
Content_zh: kalimba，這就是 kalimba。

Content: I'll play it in a second.
Content_zh: 我等一下會彈。

Content: It's a, also known as the thumb piano.
Content_zh: 它也叫拇指鋼琴。

Content: It has these little tines that you pluck with your [demonstrates].
Content_zh: 它有這些小金屬片，你可以用手指去撥。[示範]

Content: And then it has these little holes that allow you to give it sort of a vibrato.
Content_zh: 然後它有一些小孔，可以讓你做出類似顫音的效果。

Content: And that's a traditional African instrument.
Content_zh: 這是傳統的非洲樂器。

Content: [Music] McConnell: And so here you have the flute.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell： 所以這裡有笛子

Content: It's really spare.
Content_zh: 它真的很稀疏。

Content: It seemed like a lonely scene.
Content_zh: 那場景感覺很孤單。

Content: [Music] McConnell: That's sort of the whole ensemble.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：這差不多就是整個 ensemble。

Content: You hear violin, you hear, that's a kora sample, which is a…
Content_zh: 你會聽到小提琴，還有，那是一個 kora sample，是一種……

Content: McConnell: So this is a combination of the African sound and then we have kind of a European fiddle.
Content_zh: McConnell：所以這是非洲聲響的組合，然後我們也有一點歐洲 fiddle。

Content: [Music] McConnell: So that's, that's you know, that's sort of the first pass.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：所以那就是，你知道，算是第一版。

Content: It may be really different, you know, when we're finally done.
Content_zh: 當我們終於結束的時候 可能就不同了

Content: Composing for games, you know, it's a little cliche, but it's different from other media
Content_zh: 為遊戲作曲，你知道，這有點老套，但與其他媒體不同

Content: because when you write music for film, no one is, you aren't, you aren't, building the theater and redesigning the projector every time.
Content_zh: 因為你替電影寫音樂時，沒有人會每次都重新蓋戲院、重新設計放映機。

Content: With games, you pretty much are.
Content_zh: 但遊戲基本上就是這樣。

Content: You're at least redesigning the projector.
Content_zh: 至少你是在重新設計放映機。

Content: It helps to be involved in the actual technical aspect of things, not just throwing the, the music over the wall and hoping that it gets put in OK.
Content_zh: 參與實際技術面會有幫助，而不只是把音樂丟過牆，祈禱它能順利放進去。

Content: And then checking it out later.
Content_zh: 然後再查一下

Content: I mean, that's a great way to do things and sometimes it's kind of what we have to do,
Content_zh: 我的意思是，這是一個很棒。的方法 做事情，有時它是一種我們必須做的，

Content: because we're all, you know, under the schedule pressure and so on.
Content_zh: 因為我們都在排程壓力下

Content: But sometimes it's nice to be able to actually get your, roll up your sleeves, get your hands into the game.
Content_zh: 但有時候能真的捲起袖子，親手進到遊戲裡，是很好的事。

Content: Really see firsthand what's not working about it.
Content_zh: 親眼看清楚哪裡不對。

Content: Make a change and then five minutes later you know, there it is, the way you want to hear it.
Content_zh: 做個修改，五分鐘後，你知道，它就變成你想聽到的樣子。

Content: McConnell: I'm just going to see if I can actually get a signal in this system here.
Content_zh: McConnell：我只是想看看我是否真的能在這個系統裡得到信號

Content: I really have no idea what I'm going to do, actually, to be honest to be with you.
Content_zh: 老實說，我其實完全不知道自己要做什麼。

Content: But I'm, I've been trying, playing some violin into it.
Content_zh: 但我一直在努力，在拉小提琴

Content: And then I'm just going to look at a couple of other sounds I might use and see if I can make sort of a, little bit of a score here.
Content_zh: 然後我會看看其他幾個可能會用的聲音，看能不能在這裡做出一點配樂。

Content: [Music] McConnell: OK, so that's a good place to start.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：好，這是個很好的開始。

Content: [Music] McConnell: Those little sounds you hear in the background
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell： 你從背景裡聽到的聲音

Content: are things that I played on the violin already.
Content_zh: 那些是我已經用小提琴彈過的東西。

Content: I have a little bit of a synthesizer bed now.
Content_zh: 我現在有一點合成器鋪底。

Content: I'm going to play, I'm going to play some violin to it.
Content_zh: 我要跟著它拉一些小提琴。

Content: I'm not playing to a click, so this is just sort of a free, free ambient thing.
Content_zh: 我不是跟著 click 錄，所以這比較像自由的環境音。

Content: [Music] McConnell: My Prime Time is broken.
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：我的 Prime Time 壞了。

Content: Oh, nice. I think it's not broken.
Content_zh: 喔，不錯。我覺得它沒壞。

Content: And it's like, I'm not quite sure why we're not, oh.
Content_zh: 我不太確定為什麼我們沒有……喔。

Content: So I've set up a sample and hold loop.
Content_zh: 所以我設了一個 sample-and-hold loop。

Content: By plucking the violin, it's going through this old Lexcon Prime Time, which is now sampling and holding that sound and making a repeating echo out of it.
Content_zh: 我撥小提琴，聲音經過這台老 Lexicon Prime Time；它現在正在取樣並保持那個聲音，做出重複回音。

Content: And I sort of like this kind of heartbeat thing that's going on.
Content_zh: 我有點喜歡現在這種心跳感。

Content: And I've pitched it down by an octave, by messing with the, this was the original sound.
Content_zh: 我把它降了一個八度，調了這個；原本的聲音是這樣。

Content: So I'm just going to record that.
Content_zh: 所以我就把它錄下來。

Content: [Music] McConnell: And I don't know if that's what we'll use for that scene,
Content_zh: [音樂] McConnell：我不知道那是不是我們會用在那個場景的東西，

Content: but it might be something like that.
Content_zh: 但可能會是類似那樣的東西。

Content: And it's a huge score.
Content_zh: 而且這份配樂規模很大。

Content: There're three of us working on it.
Content_zh: 我們有三個人在做。

Content: So we'll be taking my ProTools files with the midi information in them, re-voiced with different synth sounds, do different remixes.
Content_zh: 所以我們會拿我的 Pro Tools 檔案，裡面有 MIDI 資訊，用不同合成音色重新配器，做不同 remix。

Content: And this is something that we're evolving, you know, we're working on it right now, exactly how it's working.
Content_zh: 這是我們正在演化的東西；我們現在正在研究它到底該怎麼運作。

Content: So it's, you know, it's definitely paving new ground.
Content_zh: 所以這絕對是在開拓新路。

Content: McConnell: You know, one thing about working hard at music is, is your head does get cluttered a lot with all the parameters and the deadlines
Content_zh: McConnell：你知道，努力做音樂有一點是，你的腦袋會被各種參數和期限塞滿，

Content: and you know, am I going to do it this way or am I going to do that way?
Content_zh: 我是要這樣做，還是那樣做？

Content: And when am I ever going to get rid of that awful synth sound I'm using?
Content_zh: 我什麼時候才能擺脫那可怕的合成音效？

Content: And all that kind of stuff, it just kind of melts away when you, when you come up here and get a little perspective on things.
Content_zh: 而這些東西，當你來到這裡、稍微拉開距離看事情時，就會慢慢融化掉。

Content: I think in some sense music is an imitation of nature or the world around us.
Content_zh: 我覺得某種意義上，音樂是在模仿自然，或我們周遭的世界。

Content: And I just really like to be here whenever I can make it up the hill.
Content_zh: 只要能爬上這座山，我就真的很喜歡待在這裡。

Content: Because it is a little reminiscent of the field that the girl starts out in, now isn't it?
Content_zh: 因為這有點讓人想到女孩一開始所在的那片田野，不是嗎？

Content: When I did that theme, we hadn't moved yet.
Content_zh: 我寫那個主題時，我們還沒搬過來。

Content: So I sort of had to imagine that field and work to the picture.
Content_zh: 所以我有點必須想像那片田野，照著畫面去創作。

Content: But you know, the idea of a, of a beautiful field with solitude that you can go to, to just get away from everything and we have a little village below,
Content_zh: 但你知道，一片美麗田野、可以獨處、可以逃離一切的地方，下面又有一個小村莊，

Content: it's definitely, you know, definitely resonates.
Content_zh: 那確實會產生共鳴。

Content: So maybe now that we're moving back to the girl's world, maybe it's time for me to take more of these walks.
Content_zh: 也許現在我們要回到女孩的世界，也許我該多出來散步。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Whoo! Full functionality. Ship it.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：嗚！功能完整。推出吧。

Content: I think we should ship it at the end of the week.
Content_zh: 我覺得我們週末就該推出。

Content: I just really want to ship it. Ship it.
Content_zh: 我真的很想推出。推出吧。

Content: Anything else? No? OK. Thanks, everybody.
Content_zh: 還有別的嗎？沒有？好，謝謝大家。

Content: [Music] Schafer: That was the most funny thing about him, is he gets,
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：他最有趣的地方就是，

Content: I've never seen anyone get madder at computers than Peter McConnell God fucking dammit!
Content_zh: 我從沒看過有人對電腦比 Peter McConnell 還火大。該死！

Content: Like he gets so, 'cause nothing else about his personality is like that way, rage-y.
Content_zh: 他會變得超火，因為他個性裡其他部分完全不是那種暴怒型。

Content: But when like a machine, when a computer won't do something, he gets so mad.
Content_zh: 但只要機器、電腦不照他的意思做，他就會超生氣。

Content: It's always fun to see him.
Content_zh: 看他這樣總是很好玩。

Content: Oh, man, I was hoping you'd capture that.
Content_zh: 喔天啊，我還希望你有拍到那個。
